mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, leading to higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate robust housing sector momentum, which amplifies broader economic demand and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a decline in the index of industrial production reflects capacity constraints and slowing manufacturing output, triggering short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to positive during late-cycle expansion periods in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 220.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of threshold-crossing months over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening labor demand and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue projections and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no persistent predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: The joint expansion of real GDP and real sales of existing homes confirms synchronized growth across production and consumption channels, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction effect becomes negative or statistically insignificant in out-of-sample predictive tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates combined with declining industrial production signal a policy-induced supply contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS rises and INDPRO declines in walk-forward validation.
